passages.passage_text:
Two statements are given below followed by two Conclusions I and II. You have to consider the two statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. You have to decide which of the conclusions, if any, follow from the given statements.
Give answer
(a) Only I follows
(b) Only II follows
(c) Both I and II follow
(d) Neither I nor II follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some cities are towns. Some villages are cities.
Conclusions: I. Atleast some villages are towns. II. No village is a town.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some pins are clips. Some clips are pens.
Conclusions: I. Some pins are pens. II. Some pins are not pens.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All Ds are As. All As are Cs.
Conclusions: I. All Ds are Cs. II. Some Ds are As.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some trees are plants. All bushes are plants.
Conclusions: I. Atleast some trees are bushes. II. Some trees are definitely not bushes.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All bottles are glasses. No cup is a glass.
Conclusions: I. No bottle is a cup. II. Atleast some glasses are bottles.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: No bangle is an earrings. Some earrings are rings.
Conclusions: I. No ring is a bangle. II. Some rings are definitely not earrings.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All seats are hot. All belts are hot.
Conclusions: I. All seats are belt. II. All hots are either seats or belts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All exams are tests. No test is a question.
Conclusions: I. Atleast some exams are questions. II. No exam is a question.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All rivers are lakes. All lakes are oceans.
Conclusions: I. All rivers are oceans. II. Atleast some oceans are lakes.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some banks are colleges. All colleges are schools.
Conclusions: I. Atleast some banks are schools. II. All schools are colleges.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some exams are tests. No exam is a question.
Conclusions: I. No question is a test. II. Some tests are definitely not exams.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some wires are fires. All fires are tyres.
Conclusions: I. Atleast some tyres are wires. II. Some fires are definitely not wires.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: No toffee is coffee. No sweet is toffee.
Conclusions: I. No coffee is sweet. II. All sweets are coffee.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some books are magazines. Some magazines are novels.
Conclusions: I. Some books are novels. II. Some novels are magazines.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All good hockey players are in the Indian hockey team. 'X' is not a good hockey player.
Conclusions: I. 'X' is not in the Indian Hockey team. II. 'X' wants to be in the Indian Hockey team.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some scales are pencils. Some erasers are pencils.
Conclusions: I. Some pencils are erasers. II. Some pencils are scales.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some chairs are made up of wood. Some tables are made up of wood.
Conclusions: I. All wooden things are either chairs or tables. II. Some chairs are tables.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All good athletes win. All good athletes eat well.
Conclusions: I. All those who eat well are good athletes. II. All those who win eat well.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some scooters are trucks. All trucks are trains.
Conclusions: I. Some scooters are trains. II. No truck is a scooter.
